Software.implementation_notes: Liquid via large-cap software ETFs or the largest 5-10 individual names. Software has the highest within-sector dispersion of any group; broad sector indexes underweight the leaders relative to cap-weight.

Semiconductors.implementation_notes: Most index ETFs cap individual stocks at 8%, which mechanically caps Nvidia's contribution. For cap-weighted exposure use SMH or XSD; for individual stock concentration build the position directly.

Biotech.gics_path: Health Care → Pharmaceuticals, Biotechnology & Life Sciences → Biotechnology
Biotech.implementation_notes: Long-duration cash flows make biotech highly rate-sensitive. Holdings are bimodal: 6-7 large profitable names versus hundreds of pre-revenue clinical-stage companies. Liquid implementation via XBI (equal-weight) or IBB (cap-weighted).

Banks.gics_path: Financials → Banks → Diversified Banks + Regional Banks
Banks.calibration_etf: KRE (regional banks) — see implementation_notes for full coverage
Banks.implementation_notes: KRE covers regional banks; KBE covers a broader bank universe. For full bank exposure including money centers, supplement with individual JPM/BAC positions.

Insurance.implementation_notes: P&C insurance benefits from rising rates (investment book yield). Life insurance benefits from steepening curves. KIE covers both; for pure P&C use IAK.

MedDevices.gics_path: Health Care → Health Care Equipment & Services → Health Care Equipment
MedDevices.implementation_notes: Less rate-sensitive than biotech. Defensive characteristics in down markets. Held via IHI for the broad exposure.

Transports.implementation_notes: Highly cyclical, sensitive to fuel costs and trade volume. Rails benefit from infrastructure spending; airlines from consumer travel demand.

AeroDefense.implementation_notes: Defense names are countercyclical to peace; commercial aerospace is cyclical to travel demand. ITA blends both. PPA is a slightly broader alternative.

OilExploration.gics_path: Energy → Oil, Gas & Consumable Fuels → Exploration & Production
OilExploration.implementation_notes: Pure-play upstream exposure. Highly volatile, levered to oil prices. For full energy sector exposure including integrateds use XLE.

Retail.implementation_notes: XRT is equal-weighted, capping mega-caps. For cap-weighted retail exposure including Amazon's full weight use RTH or XLY (broader Cons Disc).

Materials.gics_path: Materials (full sector — chemicals, metals, mining, packaging)
Materials.implementation_notes: Cyclical sensitivity to industrial demand and commodity prices. XLB is cap-weighted; for equal-weight or specific commodity exposure see RTM, GDX, COPX.

ConsStaples.implementation_notes: Defensive characteristics in stress regimes. Low volatility. XLP is the standard cap-weighted exposure.

Utilities.implementation_notes: Bond-proxy sensitivity to long Treasury yields. Defensive. Renewables theme partially captured via NEE, CEG.

RealEstate.implementation_notes: Bond-proxy plus property-cycle exposure. Heavily mortgage-rate sensitive. For specific property types (data centers, residential) see DLR, AMT, EQIX, MAA individually.

CommSvcs.implementation_notes: GICS reorg in 2018 moved Meta, Alphabet, Netflix into this sector. IYZ captures only telecom (older classification); XLC (post-2018 inception) captures the modern Comm Services. For pre-2018 back-tests we use IYZ as the deep-history proxy.

MegaCapGrowth.gics_path: Cross-sector (Tech + Cons Disc + Comm Services mega-caps)
MegaCapGrowth.implementation_notes: MGK is the cleanest single-ETF Mag-7 exposure. QQQ is broader (Nasdaq-100). XLG is cap-weighted top 50. For pure Mag-7 build the basket directly.
